rules.guidance: Flag tests with no assertions, t.Error where a nil-deref will panic before the assert is reached, or table tests that don't run subtests in a loop variable-safe way.
Flag dereference of a value that may be nil without a prior check; common with functions returning (T*, error) where the pointer is used before checking err.
Flag errors assigned to _ or ignored on calls that can fail meaningfully (I/O, parsing, network).
Flag goroutines with no exit path or missing context cancellation, and channel sends/receives that may block forever.
Flag shared mutable state accessed from multiple goroutines without synchronization (mutex, channel, atomic).
Flag context.Background()/TODO() in request paths, contexts stored in structs, or missing context propagation to downstream calls.
Flag SQL built by string concatenation or fmt.Sprintf with user input; require parameterized queries.
Flag mutable default arguments (def f(x=[]) or ={}/set()); the default is shared across calls and accumulates state. Use None and build inside.
Flag bare `except:` or `except Exception` that swallows the error without re-raising or logging, and `except` that also catches KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit.
Flag open()/socket/db connections/locks not wrapped in `with` (or not closed in finally); file and handle leaks.
Flag SQL built with f-strings, %, or .format() passed to cursor.execute; require parameterized queries (execute(sql, params)).
Flag chained-indexing assignment (df[a][b] = ...) which triggers SettingWithCopy and may silently no-op; require .loc/.iloc. Flag inplace=True relied on for a return value, and == comparisons to NaN.
Flag dereference of a nullable return (Map.get, find*, getOrNull, external API) without a null or Optional check; flag Optional.get() without isPresent.
Flag Closeable/Stream/Connection/InputStream/Reader created but not in try-with-resources or closed in finally.
Flag == / != comparing String or boxed types (Integer, Long) instead of .equals; reference equality, not value equality.
Flag empty catch blocks or catches that only printStackTrace in service/request paths, losing the failure.
Flag SQL built by string concatenation passed to Statement; require PreparedStatement with bound parameters.
Flag SELECT * in views, models, or production queries — breaks on schema drift and scans every column on columnar warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake) for direct cost.
Flag implicit cross/cartesian joins: comma-separated tables in FROM, or JOIN with no ON / always-true ON — row explosion and wrong aggregates.
Flag NULL-unsafe logic: NOT IN against a subquery that can yield NULL (returns no rows), = NULL / != NULL instead of IS [NOT] NULL, and COUNT(col) vs COUNT(*) when col is nullable.

BigQuery: flag queries over partitioned tables with no partition predicate (_PARTITIONDATE, _PARTITIONTIME, or the partition column) — full-table scan billed by bytes. Also flag SELECT * on large tables and unbounded date ranges.
Snowflake: flag scans of large tables with no pruning predicate on the cluster key, SELECT * from wide tables, and unbounded queries that defeat partition pruning (raising credits).
Flag clear logic errors, resource leaks (unclosed files/handles), missing error handling, and security issues introduced by this change.
